chunk_summary: Guidance on escalating obesity management for children and adolescents at 6–12 months, including criteria for pharmacotherapy and surgical referral, emphasizing lifestyle care and multidisciplinary support.
learning_objectives: Understand the criteria for escalating obesity management in children and adolescents at 6–12 months.
Identify when to initiate pharmacotherapy or surgical referral based on severity and comorbidities.
Recognize the importance of maintaining lifestyle interventions alongside advanced treatments.
Learn the documentation and follow-up requirements for escalation decisions.
key_facts: Adolescents aged 12+ with class 2 obesity and life-threatening comorbidities, or class 3 obesity, are eligible for pharmacotherapy after structured lifestyle treatment.
Surgical referral is recommended for children aged 12+ with class 2 obesity plus comorbidities or class 3 obesity after 6 months of failed lifestyle and pharmacotherapy.
Global guidelines emphasize early referral to prevent irreversible comorbidities.
Escalation should include a higher-contact model with dietitian, activity specialist, and psychological support.
A written plan documenting severity, comorbidities, and actions taken improves care coordination.
reference_quote: In the IAP framework, adolescents aged 12 years or older with class 2 obesity plus immediate or life-threatening comorbidities, or class 3 obesity with or without comorbidities, are eligible for adjunct pharmacotherapy as part of comprehensive care.
document_key_topics: Role of lifestyle interventions in advanced care
